skills.name: specify
skills.description: Spec-driven development — a spec is "a codebase without the code": README + trigger-keyed files describing WHAT a system does, never HOW. USE THIS AUTOMATICALLY (no command needed) whenever a project has a specs/ directory and the user asks to add/change/fix/build any behavior: consult the spec first, reflect and ask if it's unclear, implement honoring its invariants, then update the spec in the same change — the user should not have to mention specs or say "update the spec". Also use to generate specs from existing code (reverse), scaffold a spec from an idea before any code (new), build code from a spec, verify code↔spec conformance, or validate spec structure.
